title: Gaia phase-space ledger test: does S=-ln det C read real phase-space coherence?

predictions.P1: cold stream reads HIGH S on (position,velocity) grain
predictions.P2: field/halo in same volume reads LOW S
predictions.P3: S driven by position-velocity GRADIENT; survives bulk-mean removal; collapses when gradient destroyed

part_A_GD1_gaia_astrometry.data: REAL Gaia DR3 astrometry (astroquery TAP), GD1Koposov10 frame
part_A_GD1_gaia_astrometry.dimensionality: 5D: sky position + proper motion (NO RV -- GD-1 too faint for Gaia RVS)
part_A_GD1_gaia_astrometry.verdict_partA: S_stream >> S_field on the astrometric grain, BUT track-band selection manufactures corr(phi1,mu); the fake-band control shows how much. Net excess over control + the shuffle collapse are the real content. Definitive non-circular test is Part B (RV).

part_B_Orphan_S5_radial_velocity.data: REAL S5 spectroscopy (Li+2019, Vizier J/MNRAS/490/3508), OrphanKoposov19 frame
part_B_Orphan_S5_radial_velocity.verdict_partB: RV is not used to impose a phi1->RV gradient (per-field selection sets only means). S_stream >> S_field, mean-subtraction leaves S identical, gradient-removal collapses it, and the position-RV alignment is far above the permutation null. NON-CIRCULAR.
